column7: If possible, can we change default state on startup of TEC to setpoint = 21 ˚C? Temperature changes quickly enough to not need 37 on startup…
same as above, can we change this to "off on startup?"
steps 3-6 are for intializing necessary components
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "400" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump)
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "400" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump)
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "1000" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump)
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "1000" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump)
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "1500" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump)
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "1500" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump); STEPS 27-30 are a direct repeat of steps 23-26 intentionally

this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "2000" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump); STEPS 31-34 are a direct repeat of steps 23-26, and 27-30, but with slightly larger volume of liquid. All 3 batches are for washing purposes.
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "2500" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump); using air to push any remaining liquid out of relevant lines
this is equivalent to a "dispense" command with payload "3000" (i.e. eject the contents of the pump); using air to push any remaining liquid out of relevant lines (repeat w larger volume)

At this point, fluid lines should be full, or mostly full, and device should be ready to perform basic reaction setup. NOTE that we may choose to include another step at the beginning to fill the mAb fluid line, which would be a similar pattern as steps 7-10 but with port 3 and a volume of ~180 instead. For now, we may decide to add this by hand to avoid excess resource consumption.
